$comment: ADOS Reference Model Bank — Difficulty Tier records (eval facet D; docs/REDESIGN-PROPOSAL.md §4.4). Each item is scored 0-3 on four sub-axes — steps, specialisation, ambiguity, context_load — summed to a 0-12 score that maps to a tier via score_range. The tier weight (1..4) drives difficulty-weighted IQ and stratified sampling so the gold set is not all easy items (Q6). The four sub-axes live on the item (schema/extracted_item_schema.json difficulty{}); this bank defines the tiers and their weights. Validates against schema/ontology_banks.schema.json.
materiality_test: Difficulty reflects cognitive load to a CORRECT answer (steps, expertise, ambiguity, context to integrate) — not output length and not the archetype.
drift_guards: Difficulty is NOT length: a long but rote transcription is low difficulty.
Score is the sum of the four 0-3 sub-axes; tier is derived from score_range (enforced in tests, not the schema).
Stratify samples across tiers so IQ is not dominated by trivial items.
